rulings.Case Title: MARK A. BROWN VS. CALIFORNIA UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE APPEALS BOARD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Reasonable Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 29, 2019, 2018, Line 13, PETITIONER MARK BROWN's Motion For Reasonable Attorneys Fees. Petitioner Mark A. Brown's motion for an award of reasonable attorneys' fees under Code of Civil Procedure section 1021.5 is granted in the amounts and for the reasons summarized below. The criteria for a section 1021.5 award have been satisfied. The litigation resulted in the enforcement of an important right affecting the public interest, conferred a significant benefit on the general public or a large class of persons, and the necessity and financial burden of private enforcement are such as to make the award appropriate. (Code Civ. Proc. § 1021.5.) The Court of Appeal's holding in its published opinion that former employees are entitled to prejudgment interest on wrongly denied unemployment benefits at the rate of 10 percent furthers the public interest in the prompt payment of benefits, which that court recognized is "crucial to the unemployment compensation insurance system." (Brown v. California Unemployment Ins. Appeals Bd. (2018) 20 Cal.App.5th 1107, 1118; see also American Federation of Labor v. Employment Development Dept. (1979) 88 Cal.App.3d 811, 821-22 ["the upholding of unemployed workers' right to prompt payments under the Social Security Act is a significant benefit to both unemployed workers and the public at large."].) The Court's full tentative ruling was sent to Counsel by email. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Petitioner is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ITC FINANCIAL LICENSES, INC. VS. STAN SCHULDINER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Mr. Schuldiner To Accept The Gift Cards Or To Provide An Address Where The Cards Can Be Sent
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, JANUARY 29, 2019, LINE 1 PETITIONERS ITC FINANCIAL LICENSES, INC. and THE BANCORP BANK'S Motion To Compel Mr. Schuldiner To Accept The Gift Cards Or To Provide An Address Where The Cards Can Be Sent This motion will be heard in Law and Motion 302 at 9:00 AM by Judge Harold Kahn. Hearing Required. = RBM

rulings.Case Title: ITC FINANCIAL LICENSES, INC. VS. STAN SCHULDINER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent'S New Trial (Specially Set At 9:00 Am Before Judge Kahn)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 29, 2019, 2018, Line 1, Respondent'S New Trial (Specially Set At 9:00 Am Before Judge Kahn) The hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am. Cross-complainant Stan Schuldiner's motion for new trial is denied in its entirety. As Mr. Schuldiner points out, there is language in the October 17, 2018 minute order that suggests that the court granted summary judgment in favor of cross-defendants ITC Financial Licenses, Inc. and The Bancorp Bank on Mr. Schuldiner's UCL claim based on an incorrect understanding of the UCL standing requirement. The court apologizes for the sloppy language in that minute order and takes this opportunity to clarify that summary judgment was granted in favor of the cross-defendants on the UCL claim because the undisputed facts showed that Mr. Schuldiner was not entitled to restitution per the UCL, not based on standing. As explained in Kwikset Corp. v. Superior Court (2011) 51 Cal. 4th 310, 335-36, "the standards for establishing standing under section 17204 and eligibility for restitution under section 17203 are wholly distinct." The portions of Kwikset relied on by Mr. Schuldiner relate to standing, not entitlement to restitution, the only damages remedy available under the UCL. While Mr. Schuldiner likely has UCL standing per Kwikset, he is not entitled to restitution because the economic value (as opposed to the personal value that Mr. Schuldiner attributes to them) of the gift cards is the same as what he paid for them. (In re Vioxx Class Cases (2009) 180 Cal. App. 4th 116, 131). Mr. Schuldiner's arguments regarding the availability of emotional distress damages on his common law claims and his entitlement to statutory damages on his CLRA claim lack merit and the court re-affirms its decisions on those issues. A new trial motion is not an appropriate vehicle to revisit the sanctions award issued by Jude Goldsmith. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for InComm and Bancorp is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: ITC FINANCIAL LICENSES, INC. VS. STAN SCHULDINER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Defendant Itc Financial License, Inc.'S Motion To Compel Mr. Schuldiner To Accept The Gift Cards Ot To Provide An Address Where The Cards Can Be Sent (Specially Set At 9:00 Am Before Judge Kahn)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 29, 2019, 2018, Line 1, Cross-Defendant Itc Financial License, Inc.'S Motion To Compel Mr. Schuldiner To Accept The Gift Cards Ot To Provide An Address Where The Cards Can Be Sent (Specially Set At 9:00 Am Before Judge Kahn) The hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am. Cross-defendant ITC Financial Licenses, Inc.'s motion to compel Mr. Schuldiner to accept the gift cards or to provide an address where the cards can be sent is denied. While most of the reasons proffered by Mr. Schuldiner for his refusal to accept the gift cards now are likely without merit, InComm has not cited any authority that it has the right to compel Mr. Schuldiner to accept the gift cards. CCP 128(a)(4) applies to a party who is obligated on a judgment or order to perform its obligations, not to require a party to accept benefits from a judgment or order that he declines to accept. While not raised by Mr. Schuldiner, if Mr. Schuldiner accepted the gift cards, it is possible that he would be barred from appealing the judgment in favor of ITC and Bancorp, presumably something that Mr. Schuldiner wishes to avoid. (See In re Marriage of Fonstein (1976) 17 Cal. 3d 738, 744 ("settled rule that the voluntary acceptance of the benefits of a judgment will bar appeal therefrom")). While the court is sympathetic to InComm's desire to put an end to this litigation, in the absence of authority supporting the motion, the motion must be denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Mr. Schuldiner is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: PIETRO PASQUALE-ANTONIO SGROMO VS. POLYGROUP INTERNATIONAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing To Compel Arbitration Cal., Civ. Proc. Code 1281.2
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 29, 2019, 2018, Line 2, PETITIONER PIETRO SGROMO's Hearing To Compel Arbitration Cal., Civ. Proc. Code 1281.2. Petitioner Pietro Pasquale Antonio Sgromo's petition to compel arbitration is denied. Petitioner has failed to carry its burden of establishing the existence of a valid agreement to arbitrate between the parties. The Court finds on the basis of the record, including Petitioner's own declaration, that no such agreement was ever entered into. Proposed draft agreements that are exchanged but not finalized or signed cannot give rise to an enforceable arbitration agreement. (Banner Entertainment, Inc. v. Superior Court (1998) 62 Cal.App.4th 348, 359-362.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for respondent is required to prepare a proposed order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE APPLICATION OF: JEFFREY H. HAAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Of Motion To Quash And/Or Modify Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, JANUARY 29, 2019, LINE 2 PETITIONER JEFFREY HAAS' Motion To Quash And/Or Modify Subpoena Pro Tem Judge Roger Mead, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Appearance required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rmead@folgerlevin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = RBM

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL S. KECK VS. ERIC M. SAFIRE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Eric Safire'S Demurrer To Complaint Specially Set At 9am In Dept 302 Before Judge Kahn
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 29, 2019, 2018, Line 6, Defendant Eric Safire'S Demurrer To Complaint (Specially Set At 9am In Dept 302 Before Judge Kahn.) The hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am.Defendant Eric Safire's demurrer to both causes of action alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiff Michael Keck is sustained without leave to amend as to both causes of action. The face of the complaint shows that the statute of limitations on both of Mr. Keck's claims began to run no later than April 26, 2016, the day Mr. Keck obtained a default judgment against Ms. Coleman-Curry, which is more than two years before this case was filed on May 14, 2018. The two year statute of limitations in CCP 335.1 and/or CCP 339(1) applies to both of Mr. Keck's claims. Mojtahedi v. Vargas (2014) 228 Cal. App. 4th 974 fairly stands for the proposition that Mr. Keck did not have a viable claim against Mr. Safire for interference with his lien rights until the entry of the default judgment. While Mojtahedi does not discuss the statute of limitations, it nonetheless supports Mr. Keck's position that the statute of limitations did not accrue prior to 2016 notwithstanding such cases as Davies v. Krasna (1975) 14 Cal. 3d 502 and Trembath v. Digardi (1974) 43 Cal. App. 3d 834 which would support an earlier accrual date. However, per the logic of Mojtahedi, the event triggering the accrual of the statute of limitations is the entry of the default judgment, not Mr. Safire's May 12, 2016 letter responding to Mr. Keck's letter of May 9, 2016. Per the "last element accrual rule" (Aryeh v. Cannon Business Solutions, Inc. (2013) 55 Cal. 4th 1185, 1191-92), the entry of the default judgment supplied the last fact needed for Mr. Keck to maintain his claims. This is because Mr. Keck had known no later than 2012 that Mr. Safire opposed Mr. Keck's claim of any lien rights and, in all events, Mr. Keck had known no later than June 2015, when he filed what he refers to as his quantum meruit action, that Mr. Safire had interfered with his lien rights. Since at the latest Mr. Keck was able to file his claims against Mr. Safire by April 26, 2016, Mr. Keck's filing of those claims more than two years later renders them time-barred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Mr. Safire is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)
